methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $31M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $150M - $500M.

data.narrative:
CX2 Industries is a Series A defense and security company that has raised $31M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of ten companies—two public (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and eight private entities in the same sector and stage, including Stark Defence, Vatn Systems, and Ares Industries.

Two triangulation methods are applicable. The stage-progression method applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A advancement, yielding an implied valuation of $62M. The peer-median approach examines three same-sector, same-stage comparables with a median valuation of $300M and a range spanning $150M (Ares Industries) to $500M (Stark Defence), directly implying $300M.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $53M to $345M, with a triangulated median of $181M across the two methods. The wide dispersion reflects material uncertainty in stage-appropriate valuation: the stage-progression method anchors conservatively to deployed capital, while the peer-median approach reflects market pricing for defense-tech companies at Series A but with limited visibility into CX2's relative competitive position, capital efficiency, and go-to-market traction relative to named comparables. Resolution of this gap requires clarity on revenue, customer concentration, and technical differentiation versus the peer set.
